jokes.text: What have you got there? 'Don't be afraid to make a mi-' I'm not gonna beat myself up over that.
jokes.setup: Gob reading an inspirational poster about not being afraid to make mistakes
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates fear of making mistakes by stopping mid-word
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Gob's incompetence and lack of self-awareness crystallized in one beat. The irony is immediate and earned.

jokes.text: And I'm not afraid to make mistakes. Or have you forgotten this little- Damn it!
jokes.setup: Gob trying to prove he's not afraid of mistakes by referencing the poster
jokes.punchline: Immediately makes another mistake while trying to prove his point
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that reinforces the character beat. The repetition of failure makes it funnier.

jokes.text: My legs are so powerful.
jokes.setup: Context suggests Gob is doing something physical while talking
jokes.punchline: Random boast about leg strength in the middle of business discussion
jokes.explanation: Pure Gob nonsense - random and character-specific but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: 'Circumvrent' union penalties. 'Circumverate.' Circumvent. Circumvent. Means to go around. The old reach around.
jokes.setup: Gob mispronouncing 'circumvent' and Michael correcting him
jokes.punchline: Gob's sexual misunderstanding of 'reach around' in business context
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob malapropism that leads to inappropriate innuendo. Well-structured wordplay.

jokes.text: When you said you wanted photocopies... I thought that you meant you wanted them on a photo printer.
jokes.setup: Starla made tiny photocopies instead of normal-sized ones
jokes.punchline: Her logic for using a photo printer for photocopies
jokes.explanation: Solid character-based absurdist logic. Starla's incompetence is believable and specific.

jokes.text: Starla's photo printer copies - tiny photos instead of photocopies
jokes.setup: Request for photocopies
jokes.punchline: She literally used a photo printer, making tiny photos
jokes.explanation: Classic visual misunderstanding gag that's logical yet absurd

jokes.text: Flashes of Quincy. Starla claimed to have had a relationship with music legend Quincy Jones.
jokes.setup: Starla saying 'Flashes of Quincy' as if it's profound
jokes.punchline: Narrator revealing her delusional claim about dating Quincy Jones
jokes.explanation: Excellent character detail that's both absurd and specific. The narrator's deadpan delivery amplifies the absurdity.

jokes.text: Isn't he a little old for that? No. Sorry. I've been looking to get some alone time with him, and it just hasn't seemed to work out lately.
jokes.setup: Gob asking if George Michael is too old for LEGOLAND
jokes.punchline: Michael's earnest response about wanting father-son bonding time
jokes.explanation: Character-driven moment that establishes Michael's desperation for connection with his son.

jokes.text: I've got friends, Michael. I just don't talk to them because... I work all the time.
jokes.setup: Gob claiming he has friends when challenged
jokes.punchline: His weak excuse that reveals he probably doesn't have friends
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment - the pause and weak excuse reveal his insecurity while maintaining his delusion.

jokes.text: I think the plant lady's wearing a thong.
jokes.setup: Context suggests Gob is looking out the window
jokes.punchline: Random inappropriate observation about a service worker
jokes.explanation: Lazy character moment - relies on Gob being inappropriate without clever setup or payoff.

jokes.text: Please. Allow me. I'll just 'circumvrent' you.
jokes.setup: Lucille using Gob's mispronunciation
jokes.punchline: She's mocking him by adopting his malapropism
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Lucille's condescension while reinforcing Gob's earlier mistake.

jokes.text: Don't get up. I just a-find-a the supplies... because I'm a- Private detective.
jokes.setup: Gene Parmesan attempting to be stealthy
jokes.punchline: His terrible disguise and fake accent while obviously being Gene
jokes.explanation: Classic Gene Parmesan entrance - his terrible detective skills and obvious disguise are perfectly in character and genuinely surprising.

jokes.text: Gene! Oh, Gene! Isn't he the best? Gene was far from the best.
jokes.setup: Lucille's enthusiasm for Gene Parmesan
jokes.punchline: Narrator's deadpan contradiction of her assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect narrator commentary that undercuts Lucille's delusion with economical precision.

jokes.text: You get the country for free. The city-That's gonna cost ya.
jokes.setup: Gene revealing the car is in Mexico
jokes.punchline: His bizarre pricing structure for geographical information
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that perfectly captures Gene's incompetent but confident approach to detective work.

jokes.text: It's a $200 fine. Oh! I feel like such an-
jokes.setup: Gene appearing to confess to some wrongdoing
jokes.punchline: He's just talking about a parking ticket or similar mundane fine
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection that plays with audience expectations about Gene's confession.

jokes.text: I still can't believe he's going into the army. You know, he's doing it just to spite me. Then why are you throwing him a party? Just to spite him.
jokes.setup: Lucille explaining Buster's motivation and Michael questioning her logic
jokes.punchline: Lucille's equally spiteful response that mirrors Buster's motivation
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of the toxic Lucille-Buster relationship dynamic. The circular spite is absurd and character-perfect.

jokes.text: You guys have such a healthy thing going.
jokes.setup: Lucille and Buster's mutual spite described above
jokes.punchline: Michael's sarcastic assessment of their obviously dysfunctional relationship
jokes.explanation: Solid straight-man reaction that highlights the absurdity without overplaying it.

jokes.text: Right here. I counted it. Come on.
jokes.setup: Earlier Gene said he wouldn't count the money
jokes.punchline: He immediately admits he counted it
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Gene's dishonesty and lack of impulse control.

jokes.text: I think I might have someone who's going to 'cirsumvrent' the law.
jokes.setup: Gob trying to use the word circumvent again
jokes.punchline: He still can't pronounce it correctly
jokes.explanation: Running gag continuation that reinforces Gob's inability to learn or improve.

jokes.text: Ann. Here. You took this picture of us... in front of that bagel place before it was burned down. I guess I thought she was with another family.
jokes.setup: George Michael showing Michael a photo of him and his girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Michael was so oblivious he thought Ann was with strangers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment that establishes Michael's complete obliviousness to Ann while setting up the running gag of her being forgettable.

jokes.text: It's like you spend all your time with her. It's like you're hoggin' her- like you're a little Ann hog. Okay? Don't be such an Ann hog.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to express frustration about George Michael's time with Ann
jokes.punchline: The awkward portmanteau 'Ann hog' that he immediately commits to
jokes.explanation: Great made-up phrase that becomes instantly quotable. Michael's commitment to the awkward wordplay makes it funnier.

jokes.text: "Ann hog" - Michael's made-up insult
jokes.setup: Michael trying to describe George Michael monopolizing Ann's time
jokes.punchline: Creates the nonsensical term 'Ann hog'
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly awkward made-up insult that becomes iconic

jokes.text: And 8:01. Curtain has risen.
jokes.setup: Tobias responding to a question about availability
jokes.punchline: His overly dramatic way of saying he didn't get the Blue Man Group gig
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias - overwrought theatrical language for a mundane rejection. His pretentiousness is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Might as well keep this makeup on since it takes so- long to get off.
jokes.setup: Tobias explaining why he's still in blue makeup
jokes.punchline: Visual of him in blue makeup for a gig he didn't even get
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that shows Tobias's overcommitment to roles he doesn't even have.

jokes.text: First of all, we are doing this for her. Okay? Because neither one of us wants to get divorced. And 'second-of-ly,' I know you're the big marriage expert-
jokes.setup: Lindsay defending her open marriage and attacking Michael
jokes.punchline: Setting up the brutal line about his dead wife
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the following brutal punchline. Lindsay's self-justification is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Oh. I'm sorry. I forgot. Your wife is dead.
jokes.setup: Lindsay about to insult Michael's marriage expertise
jokes.punchline: Devastating reminder about his deceased wife
jokes.explanation: Brutal, perfectly-timed dark comedy. Lindsay's casual cruelty delivered with perfect economy creates maximum discomfort and surprise.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. That was 100% inappropriate, and I do apologize profusely.
jokes.setup: Lindsay's horrible comment about Michael's dead wife
jokes.punchline: Her overly formal apology that somehow makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that shows Lindsay's social ineptitude. The formal language makes the apology feel insincere.

jokes.text: Chiclet? Chiclet? You're the Chiclet! Not me! Unfortunately, in Mexican culture... this was a much more inflammatory gesture.
jokes.setup: Flashback to Michael and Gob's previous Mexico trip
jokes.punchline: Narrator explaining their innocent gesture was actually offensive
jokes.explanation: Good cultural misunderstanding gag with classic AD narrator explanation. Sets up why they can't go to Mexico together.

jokes.text: That's so you can videotape it... when they put you in a naked pyramid and point to your Charlie Browns.
jokes.setup: Lucille giving Buster a camcorder as a going-away gift
jokes.punchline: Her cruel reference to Abu Ghraib-style torture
jokes.explanation: Darkly topical humor that's perfectly in character for Lucille's cruelty. The euphemism 'Charlie Browns' makes it funnier and more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I ought to point to Uncle Oscar's Charlie Browns... next time you're on top of him, Mother.
jokes.setup: Lucille's sexual innuendo about torture
jokes.punchline: Buster firing back with sexual reference about her affair
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows Buster can be as crude as Lucille. The Charlie Browns callback works well.

jokes.text: Yes. And she's not having an affair with the boy's... uncle.
jokes.setup: Buster accusing Lucille of having an affair
jokes.punchline: Narrator's loaded pause before 'uncle' confirms the affair
jokes.explanation: Perfect narrator timing that confirms what Buster suspects through strategic hesitation. The pause does all the work.

jokes.text: Well, this, uh, is a blueprint... of Dad's face. She used the big printer. I'll give her that.
jokes.setup: Michael looking at the photo that was printed too large
jokes.punchline: The visual of a giant face printout described as a 'blueprint'
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that pays off the earlier printer confusion. 'Blueprint' is a funny way to describe a face photo.

jokes.text: He's my friend. You don't have any friends. I have lots of friends, Lindsay.
jokes.setup: Lindsay challenging Gob about his supposed friend
jokes.punchline: Gob's defensive overcompensation about having friends
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment that reinforces Gob's insecurity about friendship. Sets up the running theme.

jokes.text: In fact, the man was Ice, a bounty hunter... that Gob hired to follow Michael. Ice may be my best friend.
jokes.setup: Gob claiming to have a friend on the couch
jokes.punchline: Narrator revealing it's a hired bounty hunter, plus Gob's sad claim
jokes.explanation: Great revelation that subverts Gob's claim while making it even sadder. The fact that he calls his hired help his best friend is perfectly pathetic.

jokes.text: Ann Hog's coming? I thought it was just the two of us. Just Ann.
jokes.setup: Michael realizing Ann is coming on their trip
jokes.punchline: George Michael's confused correction
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the Ann hog nickname with good character confusion about who's coming.

jokes.text: In fact, at that moment, Buster was climbing into the trunk.
jokes.setup: Michael saying he'll have to put people in the trunk
jokes.punchline: Cut to Buster literally climbing into the trunk
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual timing and irony. The cut from Michael's joke to Buster's literal action is classic AD structure.

jokes.text: Mother, when you see this videotape, you'll know that I left. Not out of cowardice, but out of- Oh, man. It's tiring in here.
jokes.setup: Buster making a dramatic farewell video in the trunk
jokes.punchline: His heroic speech deflated by physical discomfort
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of Buster's attempted heroic moment. His physical weakness undermining his dramatic escape is perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: Mexico. In fact, he was in Santa Ana... a town six minutes inland from his own.
jokes.setup: Buster thinking he's escaped to Mexico
jokes.punchline: Narrator revealing he's barely left town
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expectations. Buster's incompetence is so complete he can't even escape properly.

jokes.text: Oh, this shall keep me safe from the hot Mexican sun.
jokes.setup: Buster putting on what he thinks is sun protection
jokes.punchline: Visual of him wearing something inappropriate while still in California
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that reinforces Buster's delusion about being in Mexico. His formal language is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Hey. Check out who's on that hog in the rearview mirror.
jokes.setup: Michael noticing someone following them
jokes.punchline: The double meaning of 'hog' (motorcycle/Ann's nickname)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels. The motorcycle/Ann connection is economical and well-crafted.

jokes.text: The Ice. Still on his tail, about 20 miles from LEGOLAND.
jokes.setup: Ice reporting to Gob about following Michael
jokes.punchline: The detail about being near LEGOLAND reinforces the family trip context
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that maintains the bounty hunter plot while referencing the family outing.

jokes.text: You're not my friend. You're a client. Gob realized that perhaps he really didn't have any friends.
jokes.setup: Gob calling Ice his friend
jokes.punchline: Ice's blunt correction leading to Gob's realization
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Gob's delusion with brutal economy. Ice's directness contrasts beautifully with Gob's neediness.

jokes.text: Buster get in trunk of car. So he's not going to the army. He must have had Michael sneak him into Mexico.
jokes.setup: Lupe reporting Buster's actions
jokes.punchline: Lucille's completely wrong conclusion about what happened
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony where the audience knows what actually happened while Lucille draws the wrong conclusion.

jokes.text: Here's some money. Go see a Star War.
jokes.setup: Lucille giving Oscar money to leave
jokes.punchline: Her hilariously wrong way of saying 'Star Wars'
jokes.explanation: Iconic Lucille line that perfectly captures her out-of-touch wealthy personality. 'A Star War' is immediately quotable and character-perfect.

jokes.text: It's just some idiot with balloons. Oh, is it? I knew it!
jokes.setup: Gene dismissing someone outside as unimportant
jokes.punchline: Lucille immediately recognizing it as significant (probably Gob)
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Gene's incompetence and Lucille's paranoid accuracy. The balloon detail is specific and funny.

jokes.text: I never thought I'd miss a hand so much. You people are just like me.
jokes.setup: Buster seeing familiar furniture in what he thinks is Mexico
jokes.punchline: His emotional declaration about finding his people (in his own house)
jokes.explanation: Beautiful irony as Buster finds kinship with people who literally have his old furniture. The hand reference is character-specific.

jokes.text: Oh, Ann? I just- You know, I barely know her. You know? Neither of us do. She's just really some girl as far as we're concerned. Right?
jokes.setup: George Michael asking what Michael thinks of Ann
jokes.punchline: Michael's awkward attempt to be casual about not knowing his son's girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy as Michael tries to downplay his ignorance about Ann while making it worse.

jokes.text: Hey. Wait a minute. Wait a minute. I know you. You're Gob's friend. Not friend. Bounty hunter.
jokes.setup: Michael recognizing Ice from the house
jokes.punchline: Ice's immediate correction about not being Gob's friend
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the friendship theme with Ice's consistent bluntness about not being Gob's friend.

jokes.text: Name's Ice. Gob hired me to stop you from fleeing the country.
jokes.setup: Michael thinks Ice is Gene Parmesan
jokes.punchline: Ice reveals he's working for Gob, not helping Michael
jokes.explanation: Good plot revelation that shows Gob's suspicions. Nice misdirection about Ice's loyalties.

jokes.text: No, this- That's actually him. He's just- He's much smaller in real life. I can see that's a man.
jokes.setup: Michael showing the giant printout of his father's face
jokes.punchline: Ice's deadpan observation about the obvious photo quality
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the oversized photo gag with Ice's professional assessment of the obvious.

jokes.text: Ye- Oh, yes. Thank you. No, I found my birth control pills. Thanks for asking, Dad.
jokes.setup: Maeby taking a phone call
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of birth control to her father while underage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Maeby rebellion - shocking her father with casual reference to mature topics.

jokes.text: Of course he will. She's a child. Well, that- that part's not really her fault.
jokes.setup: Maeby calling someone 'a child' (meaning Lindsay)
jokes.punchline: Michael thinking she means Ann and awkwardly defending her youth
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misunderstanding that works on multiple levels. Michael's awkward defense makes it funnier because he's defending the wrong person.

jokes.text: At no point... were Michael and Maeby talking about the same person. And there were only four people in their group.
jokes.setup: Extended conversation between Michael and Maeby
jokes.punchline: Narrator explaining the mathematical impossibility of their misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Meta-comedy at its finest. The narrator breaking down the logical impossibility of the misunderstanding is both funny and illuminating.

jokes.text: And it's not a race thing.
jokes.setup: Michael continuing the conversation about Ann/someone
jokes.punchline: Random declaration that suggests he's been having a different conversation
jokes.explanation: Good non-sequitur that suggests Michael has been having an entirely different conversation than we thought.

jokes.text: Oh, no. I'm going with you. I am one of you now. Ssí?
jokes.setup: Workers telling Buster they have to work
jokes.punchline: His enthusiasm for manual labor plus mispronounced Spanish
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Buster's misguided enthusiasm and cultural appropriation. The mispronounced 'Sí' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: Yeah. Great talk. That cousin of yours is a hell of a girl. Too bad you can't date her. No. I meant-Wait, you wouldn't have a problem with that?
jokes.setup: Michael talking about his conversation with Maeby
jokes.punchline: George Michael's horrified realization about potential cousin dating
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to the misunderstanding plot with George Michael's horror at the suggestion. The awkwardness is perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: Michael had just discovered he'd left his son's girlfriend in Mexico.
jokes.setup: Michael's conversation about getting to know Ann
jokes.punchline: Narrator revealing he literally left her behind
jokes.explanation: Perfect revelation that recontextualizes everything. Michael's obtuseness about Ann reaches its logical extreme.

jokes.text: if I blue myself early... I'd be nice and relaxed for a 9:00 dinner reservation.
jokes.setup: Tobias explaining his Blue Man Group preparation
jokes.punchline: The unintentional sexual double entendre of 'blue myself'
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias accidental innuendo. 'Blue myself' is perfectly innocent and completely sexual simultaneously, making it iconic.

jokes.text: Well, according to my photograph... it is. It's an honest mistake. Also, I think your knee is on my heart.
jokes.setup: Ice arresting Tobias thinking he's George Sr.
jokes.punchline: Tobias's polite complaint about the physical discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Tobias's characteristic politeness even while being violently arrested. The photo callback works well.

jokes.text: I was just hoping to have sex with you. Why am I not blacking out?
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying to explain the situation while Tobias is being choked
jokes.punchline: Tobias's practical concern about his consciousness during strangulation
jokes.explanation: Good combination of Lindsay's sexual directness and Tobias's analytical mind working even under duress.

jokes.text: This is great. We're like slave buddies.
jokes.setup: Buster working as a dishwasher with other workers
jokes.punchline: His tone-deaf comparison to slavery
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate tone-deafness from Buster, though the slavery reference feels cheap rather than clever.

jokes.text: Actually, I was sitting right there the whole time. Yeah? Way to plant, Ann.
jokes.setup: Ann revealing she was there when they thought they'd lost her
jokes.punchline: Michael's backhanded compliment comparing her to a plant
jokes.explanation: Iconic line that perfectly captures Ann's invisibility. 'Way to plant' is both a compliment and an insult, brilliantly crafted.

jokes.text: That's his girlfriend? Yeah. Is she funny or something?
jokes.setup: Gob seeing Ann for the first time
jokes.punchline: His assumption that she must have some redeeming quality he can't see
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob reaction that reinforces Ann's plainness. His confusion is genuine and insulting simultaneously.

jokes.text: Yeah, that's right, Michael. I went to our mother because I don't have any friends.
jokes.setup: Gob's elaborate scheme to spy on Michael
jokes.punchline: His vulnerable admission about his loneliness
jokes.explanation: Touching character moment where Gob's bravado drops and he admits his real problem. Well-earned emotional beat.

jokes.text: Sorry. I did see him coming. It's just... I kind of wanted to see where you were going with that.
jokes.setup: Gene appearing after Michael and Gob's emotional moment
jokes.punchline: Gene admitting he deliberately let the moment play out
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-moment where Gene breaks the fourth wall slightly. His curiosity about human emotion is both touching and funny.

jokes.text: Buster's accidental return home - newspaper delivery reveal
jokes.setup: Buster decides to never go home again
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals he was already home - they share a paperboy
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic payoff with specific visual detail (paperboy) that makes it believable

jokes.text: Wow. You really got to know her. Well, we did spend three hours together in that car ride. So, what'd you think? I don't like her.
jokes.setup: George Michael's excitement about his father bonding with Ann
jokes.punchline: Michael's blunt assessment after getting to know her
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the episode's setup about Michael getting to know Ann. The blunt honesty is both shocking and satisfying.

jokes.text: Make love in your own hand, Mother!
jokes.setup: Buster reclaiming his chair from his mother
jokes.punchline: His angry sexual insult that doesn't quite work
jokes.explanation: Good Buster line that shows his attempt at rebellion through awkward sexual aggression. The phrasing is characteristically off.

jokes.text: Thank God! A new doctor. Finally we can get a straight answer in this place. Uh, I've got some bad news. I'm Gene Parmesan. How you doin'?
jokes.setup: Tobias excited about finally getting medical help
jokes.punchline: Gene Parmesan in another terrible disguise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gene Parmesan moment combining his terrible detective skills with medical malpractice. The setup and reveal are expertly crafted.
